title: CloudKit + Supabase pulls run concurrently on login with no merge coordination — last writer wins non-deterministically
description: Both sync services pull independently on session start. If CloudKit and Supabase return different versions of the same record, the last persistToDisk() call wins. No coordination or merge between the two sync paths.
evidence: Both services triggered on login without sequencing or merge coordination
recommended_fix: Sequence syncs (Supabase first as source of truth, then CloudKit as backup) or implement a merge coordinator that reconciles both before persisting

title: lastPull advances even when rows failed decryption — failed records permanently skipped
description: When a row fails decryption in the pull loop, the empty catch block continues. lastPull timestamp still advances past the failed row. On next incremental pull, the failed row is never re-fetched.
evidence: Empty catch in row processing loop; lastPull set after loop
recommended_fix: Track failed row timestamps. Don't advance lastPull past the oldest failed row. Retry failed rows on next sync.

title: CloudKit daily log merge ignores needsSync — unsynced local edits overwritten by remote
description: mergeWeeklySnapshot() checks needsSync before accepting remote data, but daily log merge does not. A user editing a log locally (needsSync=true) loses changes when CloudKit returns an older version with a newer modifiedDate.
evidence: No needsSync guard in daily log merge path, unlike weekly snapshot merge
recommended_fix: Add the same needsSync guard from mergeWeeklySnapshot() to daily log merge

title: needsSync = false set before persistToDisk() — app kill loses sync state
description: CloudKit marks needsSync = false via direct array mutation before persistToDisk() completes. If the app is killed between the mutation and persistence, the record appears synced but the sync state is lost. Next launch will not re-push.
recommended_fix: Use markSynced() API which triggers persistence atomically, or set needsSync = false only after persistToDisk() succeeds

title: Singleton checksum computed from AES-GCM ciphertext (random IV) — skip-if-unchanged is dead code
description: pushSingletons() computes a checksum to skip unchanged records. But the checksum is computed from encrypted data, and AES-GCM uses a random IV per encryption. The same plaintext produces different ciphertext every time, so the checksum never matches. All singletons push on every sync cycle.
evidence: Checksum computed from encrypted blob, AES-GCM random IV
recommended_fix: Compute checksum from plaintext before encryption, or use a stable hash of the model's fields

title: applyRemoteSingleton() discards newer remote when local is modified — Device B changes silently lost
description: When local singleton has pending changes and remote arrives, local always wins. Device B's edits are silently discarded with no conflict notification. No lastModified comparison possible because singletons lack that field.
evidence: Local-wins policy with no timestamp comparison for singletons
recommended_fix: Add lastModified to UserProfile and UserPreferences models. Compare timestamps for conflict resolution.

title: Realtime events create unstructured Tasks that bypass .idle guard via actor reentrancy
description: Bulk server events (e.g., 30 INSERTs) each spawn a Task calling fetchChanges(). The guard case .idle check is insufficient because Swift actor reentrancy allows multiple Tasks to read .idle before any sets .syncing.
evidence: No debounce, no serialization beyond actor isolation
recommended_fix: Add a 500ms debounce on realtime events, or use an AsyncStream to serialize incoming events

title: fetchAllRecords() fires on every session change, not just first login — can overwrite local edits
description: Session change listener triggers fetchAllRecords() which resets lastPull and pulls everything. Token refresh or re-auth triggers this, potentially overwriting local edits made during the session.
evidence: Session change handler calls fetchAllRecords() unconditionally
recommended_fix: Only call fetchAllRecords() on first login (no existing lastPull). On session refresh, call fetchChanges() instead.

title: deleteAllUserRecords() clears CloudKit digests but not Supabase digests — stale digests survive deletion
description: Account deletion clears CloudKit-related UserDefaults keys but not Supabase singleton digest keys. New account's singletons share digest keys with deleted account, causing the checksum skip to fire incorrectly.
evidence: Selective UserDefaults cleanup in deletion path
recommended_fix: Clear all Supabase-prefixed UserDefaults keys during account deletion

title: CloudKit strips cardio image bytes before Supabase sync — images inaccessible on iCloud-disabled devices
description: Cardio images stored as CKAsset in CloudKit are not forwarded to Supabase. Devices without iCloud access (or new devices restoring from Supabase) permanently lose access to cardio images.
evidence: Image data in CKAsset only, not in Supabase encrypted blob
recommended_fix: Upload cardio images to Supabase Storage alongside CloudKit, or include encrypted image reference in Supabase sync

title: deleteAllUserData() partial failure leaves orphaned objects — no compensation logic
description: If Supabase storage deletion succeeds but row deletion fails (or vice versa), no rollback or retry. Orphaned objects or dangling metadata persist.
evidence: Sequential deletion without transaction or compensation
recommended_fix: Add compensation: if row deletion fails after storage deletion, log orphaned storage keys for cleanup

title: UserProfile and UserPreferences lack lastModified — singleton conflict resolution impossible
description: Without timestamps, the sync service cannot compare local vs remote to determine which is newer. Root cause of the local-always-wins policy in DEEP-SYNC-006.
evidence: No lastModified field on UserProfile or UserPreferences
recommended_fix: Add lastModified: Date? to both models, update on every mutation

title: DailyLog has no schema version — encrypted blobs cannot be migrated when model evolves
description: If DailyLog gains or removes fields, existing encrypted blobs in Supabase/CloudKit cannot be decoded. No version field to trigger migration logic.
recommended_fix: Add schemaVersion: Int = 1 to DailyLog. Check on decode, apply migration transforms for older versions.

title: cloudRecordID not persisted atomically — loss on app kill defeats CloudKit changeTag conflict detection
description: cloudRecordID is set in memory after a successful push but before persistToDisk(). If the app is killed, the record ID is lost. Next push creates a new record instead of updating, defeating CloudKit's changeTag-based conflict detection.
evidence: recordID set in memory before persistence
recommended_fix: Persist cloudRecordID as part of the same write that marks needsSync = false

title: pushPendingChanges() captures userID before async loop — concurrent sign-out pushes under invalidated auth
description: userID is captured once at the start. If the user signs out mid-push, remaining records are pushed under the old (now invalidated) auth context. The try? on session masks the 401.
evidence: userID captured before loop, try? masks session errors
recommended_fix: Re-validate session before each batch push, or check auth state between iterations
